words.name: Food
words.description:
Any nutritious substance that people or animals eat or drink, or that plants absorb, in order to maintain life and growth.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'flat O' handshape (fingertips touching thumb).
2. Tap the fingertips to the mouth/chin area once or twice.
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the 'flat O' handshape (fingertips touching thumb). 2. Tap the fingertips to the mouth/chin area once or twice.

words.name: Water
words.description:
A colorless, transparent, odorless liquid that forms the seas, lakes, rivers, and rain.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'W' handshape.
2. Tap the three fingers on the chin once or twice.
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the 'W' handshape. 2. Tap the three fingers on the chin once or twice.

words.name: Shelter
words.description:
A place giving temporary protection from bad weather or danger.
How to sign:
1. Use both hands in the 'A' or 'S' handshape (fists), held vertically, palms facing each other.
2. Tap the wrists/forearms together.
3. Arc the hands slightly over the head.
words.howToSign: 1. Use both hands in the 'A' or 'S' handshape (fists), held vertically, palms facing each other. 2. Tap the wrists/forearms together. 3. Arc the hands slightly over the head.

words.name: Sleep
words.description:
A naturally recurring state of rest for the mind and body.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the '5' handshape (open) above the face.
2. Move the hand down, closing it into a 'flat O' handshape while touching the chin/cheek area.
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the '5' handshape (open) above the face. 2. Move the hand down, closing it into a 'flat O' handshape while touching the chin/cheek area.

words.name: Safety
words.description:
The condition of being protected from or unlikely to cause danger, risk, or injury.
How to sign:
1. Use both hands in the 'S' handshape (fists), dominant hand on top of non-dominant, arms crossed slightly.
2. Swing the fists outward and down in unison.
words.howToSign: 1. Use both hands in the 'S' handshape (fists), dominant hand on top of non-dominant, arms crossed slightly. 2. Swing the fists outward and down in unison.

words.name: Health care
words.description:
The maintenance and improvement of health via the prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of illness, injury, and other physical and mental impairments.
How to sign:
1. Sign HEALTH: Use both '5' hands.
2. Tap the dominant middle finger on the shoulder, then tap the non-dominant middle finger on the chest.
words.howToSign: 1. Sign HEALTH: Use both '5' hands. 2. Tap the dominant middle finger on the shoulder, then tap the non-dominant middle finger on the chest.

words.name: Clothing
words.description:
Items worn to cover the body.
How to sign:
1. Use both hands in the '5' handshape (palms facing in).
2. Brush the hands down the sides of the chest/body repeatedly.
words.howToSign: 1. Use both hands in the '5' handshape (palms facing in). 2. Brush the hands down the sides of the chest/body repeatedly.

words.name: Urinate
words.description:
To excrete urine.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'U' handshape (index and middle fingers extended), palm facing down.
2. Move the hand forward from the crotch area.
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the 'U' handshape (index and middle fingers extended), palm facing down. 2. Move the hand forward from the crotch area.

words.name: Comfort Room
words.description:
Euphemism for restroom or bathroom.
How to sign:
1. Sign BATHROOM: Use the dominant hand in the 'T' handshape (thumb between index and middle fingers).
2. Shake the hand twice in space.
words.howToSign: 1. Sign BATHROOM: Use the dominant hand in the 'T' handshape (thumb between index and middle fingers). 2. Shake the hand twice in space.
